name: saas-ux-source-inventory
generatedFrom: SerpApi-backed exploration of official SaaS product and design-system sources

sources.title: Lightning Component Reference
evidence: Base components implement Lightning Design System designs and blueprints.
Form components support creating, editing, and viewing records.
Table and tree components display large formatted record data and hierarchy.

sources.title: Display Data in a Table with Inline Editing
evidence: Datatable is used to display Salesforce data in a table.
Inline editing lets users update field values in place.

sources.title: The basics of a board
evidence: Boards are made from groups, columns, items, and subitems.
Columns customize how board data is displayed.

sources.title: Introduction to monday.com
evidence: Views look at a single board from different angles.
Dashboards report across multiple boards using widgets and apps.

sources.title: The Dashboards
evidence: Dashboards centralize data in one location with widgets.
Quick filters and advanced filters customize dashboard data.

sources.title: Navigation patterns for Office Add-ins
evidence: Command bars house commands and navigation controls at the top of task panes.
Command bars are useful for four or more top-level sections, narrow panes, search, and filters.
Recognizable icons with tooltips and 44x44 px touch targets improve constrained UI accessibility.

sources.title: Use Google products side by side
evidence: Users can use Gmail, Calendar, Chat, Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides with side-panel apps in the same window.
Calendar, Keep, Tasks, Contacts, Voice, Maps, and add-ons can appear in the side panel.

sources.title: Insert smart chips in Google Docs
evidence: Smart chips expose calendar events and other entities inline.
Event chips can expose meeting details and join actions.

sources.title: Insert smart chips in your Google Sheets
evidence: Sheets smart chips include people, files, events, places, finance, YouTube, and ratings.
Users can narrow chip options after typing @.

sources.title: Fluent 2 React components
evidence: Fluent describes components as building blocks for specific UI problems.
Relevant components include drawer, toolbar, nav, tree, menu, tag picker, toast, message bar, persona, progress, and skeleton.

sources.title: Material Design 3 components
evidence: Material components include navigation, side sheets, chips, lists, search, snackbars, and progress indicators.
Side sheets show secondary content anchored to the side of the screen.
Chips help users enter information, make selections, filter content, or trigger actions.
